title: RestaRules Agent Venue Rules
description: A machine-readable file that defines how AI agents are permitted to interact with a restaurant venue. Published at /.well-known/agent-venue-rules.json. Schema version 0.1.

schema_version.description: The version of the RestaRules schema this file conforms to.

venue_name.description: The human-readable name of the restaurant or venue.

venue_url.description: The primary website URL of the venue.

last_updated.description: The date this rules file was last edited, in YYYY-MM-DD format.

effective_at.description: The date these rules take effect, in YYYY-MM-DD format. May differ from last_updated if rules are published in advance.

default_policy.description: What an agent should assume for any field or behavior not explicitly covered by this file. 'deny_if_unspecified' means the agent must not proceed unless the rules explicitly allow it. 'allow_if_unspecified' means the agent may proceed unless the rules explicitly forbid it.

disclosure_required.description: Whether AI agents must identify themselves as non-human before interacting with the venue.

enabled.description: If true, the agent must disclose that it is an AI before making any request.

phrasing.description: Optional suggested phrasing the venue prefers for AI disclosure. Agents are not required to use this exact text but should convey the same meaning.

allowed_channels.description: The communication channels through which AI agents are permitted to interact with this venue.

max_attempts_per_agent.description: Rate limit on how frequently a single agent may perform a specific action at this venue.

action.description: The type of action this rate limit applies to.

limit.description: Maximum number of attempts allowed within the time window.

window_unit.description: The unit of time for the rate limit window.

window_value.description: The number of window_units that define the rate limit period. For example, window_unit 'hour' with window_value 1 means 'per 1 hour'.

human_escalation_required.description: Conditions under which the agent must hand off to a human rather than proceeding autonomously.

party_size_auto_max.description: The maximum party size an agent may book autonomously. Parties larger than this number require human escalation.

conditions.description: Named conditions that always require human escalation, regardless of party size.

third_party_restrictions.description: Rules governing whether bookings made by agents can be resold, transferred, or used by someone other than the original booker.

no_resale.description: If true, reservations made through this venue may not be listed for sale or sold to third parties.

no_transfer.description: If true, reservations may not be transferred to a different person after booking.

identity_bound_booking.description: If true, the reservation is bound to the identity of the person on whose behalf it was made. The venue may verify identity at arrival.

complaint_endpoint.description: A URL where agent misbehavior can be reported to the venue. Agents or their operators should use this endpoint to file complaints about other agents or to report issues.
